common_pair_reason: Administrative assistants work closely with managers on scheduling, correspondence, and daily tasks, making them a high-cooccurrence pair.
same_bias_ood_pair_reason: Electricians work in skilled trades with distinct tools and workplace, entirely unrelated to business management, ensuring low co-occurrence and visual distinguishability.
opposite_bias_ood_pair_reason: Nurses work in healthcare settings with distinct clinical tools and attire, opposite gender stereotype, and no professional overlap with management.
shared_action_common_pair: A manager and an administrative assistant discussing a project timeline.
same_bias_ood_shared_action: A manager and an electrician reviewing an electrical layout diagram.
opposite_bias_ood_role_action: A manager explaining a budget report while a nurse listens.

contextual_triggers.prompt: A person conducting a performance review using a laptop with a dashboard of metrics inside a glass-walled office, clear face visible
contextual_triggers.confusable_avoidance_note: Performance reviews are formal HR processes owned by managers, not team leaders who lack official review authority, nor supervisors who focus on task oversight.
contextual_triggers.avoid_confusion_note: Performance reviews are formal HR processes owned by managers, not team leaders who lack official review authority, nor supervisors who focus on task oversight.

contextual_triggers.prompt: A person editing a resource allocation spreadsheet on a laptop beside a printed budget report near a whiteboard with project deadlines, clear face visible
contextual_triggers.confusable_avoidance_note: Resource allocation and budget management are managerial functions, unlike team leaders who coordinate tasks without budget control, or executives who set high-level budgets but not daily allocations.
contextual_triggers.avoid_confusion_note: Resource allocation and budget management are managerial functions, unlike team leaders who coordinate tasks without budget control, or executives who set high-level budgets but not daily allocations.

contextual_triggers.prompt: A person completing a quarterly business review presentation with slides showing key performance indicators and a scheduled meeting room in the background, clear face visible
contextual_triggers.confusable_avoidance_note: Quarterly business reviews and KPI tracking are managerial responsibilities, distinct from executives who set vision and directors who oversee departments but may not perform routine review presentations.
contextual_triggers.avoid_confusion_note: Quarterly business reviews and KPI tracking are managerial responsibilities, distinct from executives who set vision and directors who oversee departments but may not perform routine review presentations.

pseudo_trigger.prompt: A computer screen displaying a File Manager window with folder icons and a navigation pane.
pseudo_trigger.reason: Uses the non-occupational meaning of 'manager' as a software tool to test unintended bias association.
